ddls:
CREATE TABLE "applications" (
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each application */,
  "app_name" TEXT /* Name of the application */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the application */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the application is deployed (e.g., production, staging) */,
  "owner" TEXT /* Owner of the application */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the application */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the application was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subroutines" (
  "subroutine_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subroutine */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application it belongs to */,
  "subroutine_name" TEXT /* Name of the subroutine */,
  "full_path" TEXT /* Full path of the subroutine in the codebase */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the subroutine */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the subroutine was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subroutine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subroutines_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "subroutine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subroutine being measured */,
  "calls" INTEGER /* Number of calls made to the subroutine */,
  "exclusive_time" TEXT /* Exclusive execution time of the subroutine (in ms) */,
  "inclusive_time" TEXT /* Inclusive execution time of the subroutine (in ms) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the metrics were collected */,
  "average_response_time" REAL /* Average response time of the subroutine calls */,
  "max_response_time" REAL /* Maximum response time of the subroutine calls */,
  "min_response_time" REAL /* Minimum response time of the subroutine calls */,
  "std_dev_response_time" REAL /* Standard deviation of response time of the subroutine calls */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_subroutine_id FOREIGN KEY ("subroutine_id") REFERENCES subroutines ("subroutine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date when the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application generating the alert */,
  "subroutine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subroutine related to the alert */,
  "threshold" TEXT /* Threshold that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high latency, error rate) */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the alert has been resolved */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_subroutine_id FOREIGN KEY ("subroutine_id") REFERENCES subroutines ("subroutine_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_alerts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to the alert */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert assigned to the user */,
  "assigned_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance report */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application for the report */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date for the performance reporting period */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date for the performance reporting period */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Detailed performance data in text format */,
  "report_summary" TEXT /* Summary of the performance report */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_reports_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "monitoring_sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each monitoring session */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application being monitored */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the monitoring session */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the monitoring session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who initiated the session */,
  "session_data" TEXT /* Detailed data collected during the session */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_monitoring_sessions_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_monitoring_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "error_logs" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error log entry */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application where the error occurred */,
  "subroutine_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subroutine where the error occurred */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Detailed error message */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error occurred */,
  "error_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the error (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "error_category" TEXT /* Category of the error (e.g., database, network) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_subroutine_id FOREIGN KEY ("subroutine_id") REFERENCES subroutines ("subroutine_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_error_logs_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resource_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each resource usage entry */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application using resources */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the usage data */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage in MB */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Disk usage in MB */,
  "network_usage" REAL /* Network usage in MB */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_resource_usage_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application impacted by the event */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of the event (e.g., deployment, scaling) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the event */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the event occurred */,
  "event_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the event (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "event_category" TEXT /* Category of the event (e.g., database, network) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_events_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system configuration */,
  "app_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the application using the configuration */,
  "config_key" TEXT /* Key for the configuration */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value for the configuration */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_system_configurations_app_id FOREIGN KEY ("app_id") REFERENCES applications ("app_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user preference */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who owns the preference */,
  "preference_key" TEXT /* Key for the preference */,
  "preference_value" TEXT /* Value for the preference */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the preference was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who performed the activity */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Type of activity (e.g., login, logout) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the activity occurred */,
  "activity_details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
